name: Spiderweb Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 box (18.5 ounces) chocolate cake mix, batter prepared according to package directions
1 tube black decorating gel or 1 string black licorice
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Coat two 8-inch cake pans with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Pour the cake batter into the pans and bake for 28 to 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
4. Invert one cake onto a serving platter while still warm. Top with mini marshmallows and place the second cake layer right side up over the marshmallows. Let sit for 5 minutes for the marshmallows to melt, then chill until firm.
5. In a small bowl, mix together white frosting with red and yellow food colors until orange. Frost the top and sides of the cake.
6. Place remaining white frosting into a resealable plastic storage bag. Cut a very small tip off the corner of the bag and pipe a spiderweb design over the top of the cake.
7. Place black jelly beans on the spiderwebs, drawing legs with the gel or forming them with licorice to resemble spiders. Repeat for the second spider.
